findings.title: Test code in production: 1 issue
findings.description:
Test utilities and patterns found in what appears to be production code.

**Found:**
• Debug flags (line 609)
⚠️ This file is imported by production code!
findings.suggested_fix:
Options:

1. **Move to test files:** If this is test code, move it to a test directory
2. **Use TYPE_CHECKING:** For type hints only, wrap imports in `if TYPE_CHECKING:`
3. **Environment check:** If needed at runtime, guard with environment variable

```python
# Instead of:
from unittest.mock import Mock

# Use:
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
if TYPE_CHECKING:
from unittest.mock import Mock
```
findings.why_it_matters: This test code is imported by production code. Test dependencies add bloat, security risks, and can cause unexpected behavior in production.

findings.title: Long method: Run (583 lines)
findings.description: [COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] Function 'Run' has 583 lines (effective threshold: 150).
findings.suggested_fix: Break into smaller, focused functions.
findings.why_it_matters: Long methods are hard to understand, test, and maintain. Each function should do one thing well.

findings.title: Long method: parseOptions (1007 lines)
findings.description:
Function 'parseOptions' has 1007 lines (effective threshold: 150).

**Graph Analysis:**
High complexity density: 0.37 (complexity 374 / 1007 lines)
findings.suggested_fix: Break into smaller, focused functions.
findings.why_it_matters: Long methods are hard to understand, test, and maintain. Each function should do one thing well.

findings.title: Mutual recursion: 15 functions in call cycle (complexity 1010)
findings.description: Mutual recursion detected: 15 functions form a call cycle. Cycle: /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/fzf/src/core.go::Run:54 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/fzf/src/proxy.go::fifo:26 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/fzf/src/proxy.go::runProxy:36 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/fzf/src/proxy_windows.go::mkfifo:36 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/fzf/src/terminal.go::(*Terminal).Loop:5579 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/fzf/src/terminal.go::(*Terminal).captureLine:5081 ... (+9 more). Aggregate complexity: 1010.
findings.suggested_fix: Break the cycle by extracting shared logic into a common helper, introducing an event system, or restructuring the call chain to be unidirectional.
findings.why_it_matters: Mutual recursion creates tight coupling between functions, making them impossible to understand, test, or refactor independently. It can also cause stack overflows if the recursion depth is unbounded.

findings.title: Single point of failure: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/fzf/src/core.go::Run:54` dominates 247 functions (28%)
findings.description: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/fzf/src/core.go::Run:54` dominates 247 of 883 functions (28.0%). PageRank percentile: 98%. Blast radius boundary: /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/fzf/main.go::exit:44, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/fzf/src/ansi.go::(*ansiState).colored:32, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/fzf/src/ansi.go::extractColor:260, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/fzf/src/cache.go::NewChunkCache:18, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/fzf/src/chunklist.go::(*ChunkList).Push:83 (+127 more)
findings.suggested_fix: Consider splitting this function into smaller, independently callable units. Extract sub-functionality behind interfaces so callers have alternative paths.
findings.why_it_matters: A single point of failure means all dependent code paths funnel through one function. If this function has a bug, performance issue, or API change, the blast radius is disproportionately large.

findings.title: Long method: Loop (2140 lines)
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] Function 'Loop' has 2140 lines (effective threshold: 150).

**Graph Analysis:**
High complexity density: 0.33 (complexity 713 / 2140 lines)
Calls 3 different modules - possible split points
findings.suggested_fix:
This function calls 3 different modules. Consider extracting:
  - `handle_src()` for src operations
  - `handle_tui()` for tui operations
  - `handle_util()` for util operations
findings.why_it_matters: Long methods are hard to understand, test, and maintain. Each function should do one thing well.

findings.title: Excessive nesting: 13 levels in Loop
findings.description:
13 levels of nesting (threshold: 7).

**Analysis:**
In function: `Loop`
High complexity: 713 (nesting compounds this)
Existing helpers that could reduce nesting: override, previous, trackCurrent

Threshold: 5 (adaptive) | Actual: 13 | Default would be: 5 | Profile: p90=3, p95=4, mean=1.2, n=746
findings.suggested_fix:
This function already has helpers like `override`. Consider:
1. Extract more nested blocks into similar helpers
2. Use guard clauses (early returns) to reduce nesting
3. Replace nested ifs with switch/match
findings.why_it_matters: Deep nesting makes code hard to read and maintain. Each level increases cognitive load exponentially.

findings.title: Long method: escSequence (456 lines)
findings.description:
Function 'escSequence' has 456 lines (effective threshold: 150).

**Graph Analysis:**
High complexity density: 0.41 (complexity 189 / 456 lines)
findings.suggested_fix:
High complexity density - this function does too much logic.
1. Extract conditional branches into helper functions
2. Use early returns to reduce nesting
3. Consider the Strategy pattern for varying behaviors
findings.why_it_matters: Long methods are hard to understand, test, and maintain. Each function should do one thing well.

findings.title: Unreachable code after return
findings.description:
Code after return/throw/exit will never execute:
```
Loop:
```
findings.suggested_fix: Remove unreachable code or fix control flow logic.
findings.why_it_matters: Unreachable code indicates logic errors and adds confusion.

findings.title: Complexity Outlier: Run
findings.description: [COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] Function 'Run' is a statistical complexity outlier (z-score: 4.0, complexity: 131).
findings.suggested_fix: Review and refactor - consider breaking into smaller functions
findings.why_it_matters: Complexity outliers indicate functions that need review and decomposition

findings.title: Long method: parseKeyChords (306 lines)
findings.description:
Function 'parseKeyChords' has 306 lines (effective threshold: 150).

**Graph Analysis:**
High complexity density: 0.48 (complexity 147 / 306 lines)
Calls 3 different modules - possible split points
findings.suggested_fix:
This function calls 3 different modules. Consider extracting:
  - `handle_tui()` for tui operations
  - `handle_util()` for util operations
  - `handle_src()` for src operations
findings.why_it_matters: Long methods are hard to understand, test, and maintain. Each function should do one thing well.

findings.title: Long method: resizeWindows (548 lines)
findings.description:
Function 'resizeWindows' has 548 lines (effective threshold: 150).

**Graph Analysis:**
Orchestrator pattern: calls 31 functions (reduced severity)
Calls 3 different modules - possible split points
findings.suggested_fix:
This appears to be an orchestrator function (coordinates many calls).
If it must remain long, ensure it:
1. Has clear section comments
2. Handles errors at each step

findings.title: Excessive nesting: 9 levels in Run
findings.description:
9 levels of nesting (threshold: 7).

**Analysis:**
In function: `Run`
Entry point/handler (reduced severity)
High complexity: 131 (nesting compounds this)
Contains 2 match/switch level(s) (discounted from raw depth 10)
Existing helpers that could reduce nesting: bumpMajor, bumpMinor, buildItemTransformer

Threshold: 5 (adaptive) | Actual: 9 | Default would be: 5 | Profile: p90=3, p95=4, mean=1.2, n=746
findings.suggested_fix:
This function already has helpers like `bumpMajor`. Consider:
1. Extract more nested blocks into similar helpers
2. Use guard clauses (early returns) to reduce nesting
3. Replace nested ifs with switch/match
findings.why_it_matters: Deep nesting makes code hard to read and maintain. Each level increases cognitive load exponentially.
